task_description: Screen each abstract for a systematic review on ML methods for automated seizure or epilepsy detection from EEG signals. INCLUDE a paper only if it presents or evaluates an ML model that detects seizure events or classifies epilepsy from EEG recordings. Papers about seizure prediction (forecasting future seizures), post-ictal classification, treatment outcome, SEEG localisation, or non-EEG biomarkers should be EXCLUDE.

papers.title: Bidirectional GRU for Automated Focal Seizure Detection in Scalp EEG
papers.abstract: We train a bidirectional GRU on 3-second EEG windows from 180 patients in the Temple University Hospital EEG Corpus to detect focal seizure events. Sensitivity 0.84 and specificity 0.88 are achieved, outperforming a 1D CNN and an SVM with handcrafted spectral features. We evaluate across four seizure types and provide per-type performance breakdowns.
papers.topic_hint: GRU, scalp EEG, focal seizure detection, Temple University EEG Corpus
papers.methodology_hint: biGRU, TUHSZ, sensitivity 0.84, specificity 0.88, CNN and SVM baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: BiGRU for focal seizure detection, sensitivity 0.84, 3 baselines, per-type breakdown.

papers.title: Transformer for Predicting Seizure Occurrence from Interictal EEG Features
papers.abstract: Seizure forecasting from interictal EEG could enable patient-specific warning systems. We train a transformer on spectral features extracted from 72-hour iEEG recordings to predict seizure occurrence probability in the next 4 hours. AUC 0.79 is achieved on held-out patients. This is a pre-ictal forecasting task, not detection of seizures already occurring.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, seizure forecasting, interictal EEG, pre-ictal prediction
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 72h iEEG, AUC 0.79 — seizure forecasting not detection of ongoing seizures
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for seizure occurrence forecasting, AUC 0.79, 4-hour horizon, not detection.

papers.title: Convolutional Neural Network for Neonatal Seizure Detection from EEG
papers.abstract: Neonatal seizures are clinically subtle and difficult to detect manually. We train a multi-channel CNN on 80 hours of annotated neonatal EEG from the Helsinki University Hospital dataset to detect electrographic seizure patterns. Sensitivity 0.87 at false alarm rate 0.6/hour is achieved, substantially outperforming a previous LSTM and rule-based detector baselines.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, neonatal EEG, seizure detection, Helsinki University Hospital
papers.methodology_hint: multi-channel CNN, Helsinki neonatal EEG, sensitivity 0.87, LSTM and rule-based baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for neonatal seizure detection, sensitivity 0.87, outperforming LSTM and rule-based.

papers.title: Machine Learning for Identifying Epileptic Foci from SEEG for Surgical Planning
papers.abstract: Stereo-EEG is used to localise seizure onset zones before resective surgery. We develop an ML model to rank electrode contacts by epileptogenicity from SEEG recordings. Concordance with post-surgical outcome in 62 patients is 0.71 AUC. This localises seizure generators for surgical planning; it does not perform automated seizure event detection.
papers.topic_hint: SEEG, epileptic focus localisation, surgical planning, epileptogenicity
papers.methodology_hint: ML, SEEG, epileptogenicity ranking, AUC 0.71 — localisation for surgery not seizure detection
papers.claimed_contribution: ML for SEEG epileptic focus localisation, AUC 0.71, surgical planning not seizure detection.

papers.title: Sparse Autoencoder for Epileptiform Discharge Detection in Routine EEG
papers.abstract: Routine EEG reports epileptiform discharges (IEDs) as markers of epilepsy. We train a sparse autoencoder on 14,000 annotated EEG segments from 420 adult patients to detect IEDs including spikes, sharp waves, and spike-wave complexes. F1 0.81 is achieved, outperforming a CNN and a Hilbert-transform rule baseline. IED detection differs from seizure detection; these are interictal markers.
papers.topic_hint: sparse autoencoder, epileptiform discharges, routine EEG, IED detection
papers.methodology_hint: sparse autoencoder, 14k EEG segments, F1 0.81, CNN and Hilbert baselines, IED not seizure detection
papers.claimed_contribution: Sparse autoencoder for IED detection F1 0.81, EEG epilepsy marker classification.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Predicting Anti-Epileptic Drug Treatment Outcome
papers.abstract: Predicting which patients will achieve seizure freedom on first-line anti-epileptic drugs could personalise therapy. We train a neural network on clinical features and EEG spectral markers from 1,800 newly diagnosed epilepsy patients to predict treatment response. AUC 0.72 is achieved. This predicts drug response, not seizure events in EEG data.
papers.topic_hint: neural network, AED treatment outcome, epilepsy, drug response prediction
papers.methodology_hint: NN, 1800 epilepsy patients, AUC 0.72, drug response — treatment outcome not seizure detection
papers.claimed_contribution: NN for AED treatment response prediction, AUC 0.72, not EEG seizure detection.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for Seizure Type Classification from 10-20 Scalp EEG Topographies
papers.abstract: Different seizure types require different clinical management. We represent multichannel EEG as functional connectivity graphs and train a GNN on 2,600 seizure segments from the TUSZ corpus to classify generalised tonic-clonic, focal onset impaired awareness, and absence seizure types. Macro-F1 0.83 is achieved, outperforming spectral feature SVM and per-channel CNN baselines.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, seizure type classification, EEG connectivity, TUSZ corpus
papers.methodology_hint: GNN, TUSZ 2600 segments, macro-F1 0.83, SVM and per-channel CNN baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for EEG seizure type classification macro-F1 0.83, functional connectivity features.

papers.title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving EEG Abnormality Classification Across Hospitals
papers.abstract: We implement federated learning to train an EEG abnormality classifier across eight hospitals without sharing patient recordings. The federated model classifies EEGs as normal, epileptiform, or artefactual, achieving macro-AUC 0.87. We note the model classifies EEG abnormality patterns including epileptiform activity; it does not detect ictal seizure events in real time.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, EEG abnormality, epileptiform classification, multi-site
papers.methodology_hint: federated learning, 8 hospitals, macro-AUC 0.87, EEG abnormality — not ictal seizure detection
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated EEG abnormality classifier macro-AUC 0.87, includes epileptiform classification.

papers.title: 1D ResNet for Real-Time Generalised Seizure Detection in Long-Term EEG Monitoring
papers.abstract: Long-term EEG monitoring generates large volumes requiring automated seizure detection. We develop a 1D ResNet trained on 450 hours of long-term monitoring EEG from 92 patients to detect generalised tonic-clonic seizures in near-real-time. Sensitivity 0.91 at 0.3 false alarms per hour is achieved, with inference time under 50 ms per 2-second window.
papers.topic_hint: 1D ResNet, long-term EEG monitoring, seizure detection, generalised tonic-clonic
papers.methodology_hint: 1D ResNet, 450h long-term EEG, sensitivity 0.91, 0.3 FA/hour, real-time inference
papers.claimed_contribution: 1D ResNet for real-time GTCS detection, sensitivity 0.91, 0.3 false alarms/hour.

papers.title: Multivariate LSTM for Post-Ictal Autonomic Recovery Monitoring After Generalised Seizures
papers.abstract: Post-ictal autonomic dysregulation contributes to sudden unexpected death in epilepsy. We train an LSTM on ECG, SpO2, and nasal airflow signals recorded immediately after generalised tonic-clonic seizures to characterise recovery trajectories. Patients with prolonged autonomic suppression are identified. This is a post-ictal monitoring study, not seizure event detection from EEG.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, post-ictal, autonomic recovery, ECG, SUDEP
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM, post-ictal ECG+SpO2, recovery trajectories — post-ictal monitoring not seizure detection
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM for post-ictal autonomic recovery characterisation, SUDEP risk, not seizure EEG detection.

papers.title: Attention-Based CNN for Online Seizure Detection in ECoG Implant Data
papers.abstract: Closed-loop neuromodulation requires real-time seizure detection from intracranial recordings. We train an attention CNN on subdural ECoG strips from 24 patients with drug-resistant focal epilepsy to detect seizure onset in real time. Sensitivity 0.89 at 0.2 false detections per hour is achieved. Latency from seizure onset to detection is under 4 seconds.
papers.topic_hint: attention CNN, ECoG, intracranial EEG, seizure detection, closed-loop
papers.methodology_hint: attention CNN, ECoG 24 patients, sensitivity 0.89, 0.2 FA/hour, 4s latency
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention CNN for ECoG seizure detection, sensitivity 0.89, 4s detection latency.

papers.title: Random Forest for Discriminating Epileptic from Non-Epileptic Attack Disorder in EEG
papers.abstract: Distinguishing epileptic seizures from non-epileptic attack disorder (NEAD) is clinically challenging. We extract time-frequency features from ictal EEG and train a random forest to classify recorded attacks as epileptic or NEAD. Accuracy 0.81 is achieved. We note this classifies attack type from existing EEG recordings, not prospective seizure detection.
papers.topic_hint: random forest, epilepsy vs NEAD, EEG classification, attack type
papers.methodology_hint: random forest, ictal EEG features, accuracy 0.81, epileptic vs NEAD classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Random forest for epileptic vs NEAD classification from EEG, accuracy 0.81.

papers.title: Graph-Based Feature Extraction for EEG-Based Anaesthesia Depth Monitoring
papers.abstract: Monitoring anaesthesia depth prevents intraoperative awareness. We extract graph-based connectivity features from intraoperative EEG and train an SVM to classify anaesthesia depth levels. The work is about anaesthesia monitoring; no seizure or epilepsy classification is performed.
papers.topic_hint: SVM, EEG, anaesthesia depth, intraoperative monitoring
papers.methodology_hint: SVM, intraoperative EEG connectivity, anaesthesia depth — not seizure or epilepsy detection
papers.claimed_contribution: SVM for anaesthesia depth monitoring from EEG, not seizure detection.

papers.title: Transformer Encoder for Detecting Subclinical Seizures in Critically Ill Patients
papers.abstract: Subclinical seizures in the ICU are detectable only by continuous EEG and are missed by clinical observation. We fine-tune a transformer encoder on 920 hours of continuous EEG from 110 critically ill patients to detect subclinical electrographic seizures. Sensitivity 0.86 and specificity 0.91 are achieved, outperforming a threshold-based rule system and a prior CNN baseline.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, subclinical seizures, continuous EEG, ICU, critically ill
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 920h EEG, 110 ICU patients, sensitivity 0.86, specificity 0.91, CNN and rule baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for ICU subclinical seizure detection, sensitivity 0.86, 2 baselines.

papers.title: Bayesian Deep Learning for Uncertainty-Aware Sleep Stage Classification from PSG
papers.abstract: Sleep stage classification from polysomnography informs sleep disorder diagnosis. We train a Bayesian CNN on 5,000 PSG recordings to classify five sleep stages, achieving accuracy 0.82. Uncertainty estimates flag ambiguous epochs for expert review. This is sleep stage classification from PSG; EEG seizure detection is not performed.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian CNN, sleep staging, PSG, uncertainty estimation, sleep disorders
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian CNN, 5000 PSG, accuracy 0.82, sleep staging — not seizure or epilepsy detection
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian CNN for sleep staging from PSG, accuracy 0.82, uncertainty for ambiguous epochs.
